reasoning: While the fidelity score is high (0.89), the document is a formal government security policy where the omission of versioning metadata and effective dates represents a significant audit risk. Furthermore, the corruption of nested list structures in section PE-17 and the pipeline's 'needs_retry' status indicate structural failures that require human correction to ensure the policy remains legally and technically sound.

concerns.description: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
concerns.analysis: The output document fails to include critical document metadata found in the header of every page of the source PDF. This includes the 'Document No. (SCIO-SEC-313)', 'Effective Date (01/29/2018)', 'Review Date (03/26/2025)', and 'Version (4)'. While the body text of the policy is accurate, the omission of these identifying dates and version numbers is a defect for a government policy document where auditability is key.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Document No. SCIO-SEC-313, Effective Date 01/29/2018, Review Date 03/26/2025, Version 4

concerns.analysis: In section PE-17 (Alternate Work Site), the list structure is corrupted. Sub-items 'ii' through 'vi' (which are part of the nested list under item 'd' on Page 12) are incorrectly extracted as top-level sibling items to 'd'. In the source, item 'd' begins on Page 11 and its sub-items continue onto Page 12. The output fails to preserve this nesting across the page break.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: d. Alternate work sites must meet state and federal security control requirements... The following shall be implemented for alternate work sites: i. Sufficient physical access... ii. Design requirements... iii. Equipment being used... iv. Equipment transported... v. Equipment must not... vi. NIST SP 800-46...
concerns.output_quote: { "text": "d. Alternate work sites must meet state and federal security control requirements...", "nested_list": { "items": [ { "content": [ { "text": "i. Sufficient physical access..." } ] } ] } }, { "content": [ { "text": "ii. Design requirements..." } ] }, { "content": [ { "text": "iii. Equipment being used..." } ] }
concerns.location: PE-17 – Alternate Work Site, Page 11 and 12

concerns.analysis: Content from the page headers in the source PDF (specifically the NC State Seal and the policy title) is being duplicated and injected into the body of the HTML document at what would be page breaks. For example, the seal and the title 'Physical and Environmental Protection Policy' appear again just before the PE-1 section, and the seal appears again before PE-3. These elements should only appear once at the top of the document in a continuous HTML flow.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The Great Seal of the State of North Carolina and 'Physical and Environmental Protection Policy' text appear in the header of every page (e.g., Page 2, Page 4).
concerns.output_quote: The NC State Seal and the title 'Physical and Environmental Protection Policy' are repeated between the 'Responsibilities' table and 'PE-1'. The seal is repeated again before 'PE-3'.
concerns.location: In HTML output, preceding section headers for PE-1 and PE-3.

action_items: Restore document metadata (Document No, Version, Dates) to the document head or header section.
Correct the nesting hierarchy for the list in section PE-17 (item d).
Remove duplicated header elements (seals and titles) from the body of the HTML.
